item.title: For those staying safe while exercising our human rights
item.description: Thermal gloves (kevlar alone won't cut it), closeable waterproof sack (not plastic, not rubber), full face respirators are available online or at hardware stores (needs a charcoal filter for teargas, look for the pink filters and bring extra filters) Motorcycle style gear wouldn't be a bad idea with the trigger happy pigs running around, thick dense full coverage will help disperse impact of rubber rounds. Stay furious my friends
item.dc:creator: theSoulciologist

item.title: More amazing funny things. Enjoy !
item.description:
Blondie wanna be next
Round and round and round he goes
Walking in the morning sun.
Removing ice from water
seems a good idea ... but reminds me of sumthing.
OSHA would have a stroke.
Handling this 40-foot behemoth
That’s actually impressive.
The paperwork speedrun. This guy is some kind of a hero !
This dude’s palms were soaked !
Helicopter makes emergency landing in India. Looks like it tries to reproduce on that poor car ...
Portable Camping Shower.
Homemade transport with wide front wheels and a small rear mount engine.
What a cool guy ... more transport.
Where does one even start with contructing this ??
Flipping oyster pods
This is amazing ... Instinctive drawing.
Ox-driven oil mill (ghani)

item.title: Might want to use the law while we still got it...
item.description: God bless the resistance in LA..... 1) "Am I free to leave?" It's worth asking this even if the answer is obvious. Even if the officer does not let you leave, by forcing them to admit that you are not free to leave, you are creating a record which your attorney can use to prove that you were in custody. Miranda rights only apply if the interrogation is custodial, meaning that police officers will frequently claim that their suspects were "not in custody" to get around their Miranda rights. 2) "I am invoking my right to remain silent." Simply staying silent will not invoke your right to remain silent. As absurd as this is, you must explicitly say that you are invoking your right to remain silent in order to invoke that right. 3) "I am invoking my right to an attorney." As stated above, you must be not only clear and unambiguous, but clear and legally unambiguous. Don't get cute. Don't get sassy. And on the flip side, don't get intimidated and use verbal ticks to minimize your request. Say the line with those words exactly - say it clearly, and say it once, and then say nothing else. Shut the f*** up Because even after you've done all this, the police can still try to get you to talk. They're not supposed to interrogate you, but they're allowed to make casual conversation, and if that conversation just happens to circle back around to the thing they wanted to question you about, well, that's really your fault for talking after you said you wouldn't, isn't it?

item.title: Raleigh NC, peaceful and escorted by the police in their path around town keeping the roads open for the walk.
item.description: A couple things. When the walk was over I over heard two kids talking crap about the cops. This bothers me because this is the time we need to work together and support each other. Also I overheard someone say, that there always seems to be a kind of people who do these things ( participate in protests). This really made me realize how sheltered some people are who do not realize, yes, it is because of these kind of people that at some point in history we gained the rights to protect from discrimination, reproductive choices, abortions and the ability to marry whom we want no matter the gender. And it is partly because of the people who remain silent that we are losing these rights. Also came to really recognize that if we want our rights back, it will be a forever journey to keep and maintain them after having to fight to get them back.
item.dc:creator: MustardNTots

item.title: He came out of retirement to warn us about a grave danger
item.description:
This is the legendary Sir David Attenborough. Renowned biologist and natural historian. He has been loved by millions of people for this enlightening, wonderous documentaries about so many facets of nature on Earth.
He has seen so much across his lifetime. What initially prompted him to come out of retirement was the astounding progress made in visual recording resolution and miniaturization. It opened up the world of nature to profoundly new levels, enabling us to see so much more than ever before. He couldn't pass up the chance to get back into it, and so we became very fortunate to enjoy his return to documentaries.
But there is another more pressing reason why David Attenborough is working so hard to produce as many nature related documentaries as he can before he becomes physically unable to do so. A WARNING to US ALL.
Climate change is real. It's happening. And, it's not something that has happened as a purely natural course of Earth's cycles. No. In fact, there's hard evidence that human beings with a global scale of massive industrial pollution have been largely responsible. And the unnerving problem is, that TOO MANY PEOPLE either do not believe it's happening ("climate hoax") or they don't feel there's an urgent danger. Life right now feels mostly normal, except for more highly damaging storms, droughts, and floods in various parts around the world. The insidious thing about global warming is that it's like high blood pressure. You have it happening, but you otherwise feel fine... until a catastrophic moment. And by then, it's too late.
David Attenborough: ‘Act now on climate change or it will be too late’ - BBC News https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vgnAMrp5_wM Sir David Attenborough Shares Words of Wisdom https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g0VXvrNAMUs
David Attenborough takes a stark look at the facts surrounding climate change in today's world, detailing the dangers we are already having to deal with and future threats, but also the possibilities for prevention and radical political, social and cultural change. https://www.imdb.com/title/tt10095266/?ref_=ext_shr_lnk "If we don't act now, it'll be too late." That's the warning from Sir David Attenborough ahead of the COP26 climate summit in Glasgow. The broadcaster says the richest nations have "a moral responsibility" to help the world's poorest. And it would be "really catastrophic" if we ignored their problems, revealed in a BBC News interview. "Every day that goes by in which we don't do something about it is a day wasted," he said. And Sir David said that proved that he and others had not been making "a fuss about nothing", and that the risks of a hotter world are real. https://www.bbc.com/news/science-environment-59039485
item.dc:creator: cytherians

item.title: What Happened Today #131
item.description: ? Trump spent today laying the groundwork for his next wave of crackdowns. He announced plans to ship 9,000 people to Guantanamo and eliminate FEMA while calling protesters "insurrectionists", the exact word that justifies invoking wartime emergency powers against American citizens. He even told soldiers at Ft. Bragg that he was liberating Los Angeles. DOJ reversed an 87-year-old precedent to let Trump abolish national monuments like the two Biden established, while his systematic destruction of health and science continued with the NIH director openly accepting responsibility for $9.5 billion in research cuts and HHS officials attacking the health industry. Trump built his rationale today for bigger authoritarian actions tomorrow. The language, the planning, the institutional preparation all points to what's coming next. Relishing the fight, he even warned that any protesters at his birthday parade will be "met with heavy force." Don't wait for the next wave to hit. Get busy today. Meanwhile, don't rely on the algorithm to keep you informed. Let me send this free and private summary to you every night so that you never miss what's going on. Add your email on the homepage of GovBrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/us-china-respite 2. https://go.govbrief.today/tariff-ruling-paused 3. https://go.govbrief.today/cfpb-official-quits 4. https://go.govbrief.today/montana-abortion-unconstitutional 5. https://go.govbrief.today/louisiana-targets-doctors 6. https://go.govbrief.today/nih-director-testifies 7. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-abolish-monuments 8. https://go.govbrief.today/la-protests-insurrection-act 9. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-fort-bragg 10. https://go.govbrief.today/protests-other-cities 11. https://go.govbrief.today/thousands-to-guantanamo 12. https://go.govbrief.today/confederate-names-bases 13. https://go.govbrief.today/va-hires-opm 14. https://go.govbrief.today/mciver-indicted 15. https://go.govbrief.today/ice-raids-omaha-plant 16. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-wins-due-process-pause 17. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-fema-elimination 18. https://go.govbrief.today/opm-broke-law-data 19. https://go.govbrief.today/abc-fires-moran 20. https://go.govbrief.today/means-conflict-health-industry Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/doge-tracker-update Yesterday's Story - https://go.govbrief.today/cnn-reporter-detained Subscribe Free & Private - https://govbrief.today [Imgur reminder-I can see private messages, but can't reply--still]
